steps.title: Use the value at zero
steps.content: For an exponential function $g(x)=a(b)^x$, the value at $x=0$ is $a$ because $b^0=1$. The table shows $g(0)=1$, so $a=1$.

steps.title: Use the next table value
steps.content:
With $a=1$, the model is $g(x)=b^x$. The table shows
$$g(1)=\frac{1}{25}$$
so
$$b=\frac{1}{25}$$

steps.title: Write the function
steps.content:
Therefore, the function is
$$g(x)=(\frac{1}{25})^x$$
The negative choices have the wrong sign, and $25^x$ has the wrong base. The answer is D.
